questions.q: By what famous screen persona was the Hindi film actor Amitabh Bachchan known during the 1970s?
questions.explanation: Amitabh Bachchan rose to fame as the brooding 'Angry Young Man' of 1970s Hindi cinema.

questions.q: In which 1973 film directed by Prakash Mehra did Amitabh Bachchan achieve his breakthrough as a fiery police inspector?
questions.explanation: Zanjeer (1973) gave Amitabh Bachchan his breakthrough role as Inspector Vijay Khanna and launched the angry young man image.

questions.q: In which year was the Indian actor Amitabh Bachchan born?
questions.explanation: Amitabh Bachchan was born on 11 October 1942 in Allahabad.

questions.q: In the 1975 blockbuster Sholay, which character did Amitabh Bachchan play?
questions.explanation: Amitabh Bachchan played the quiet, loyal Jai alongside Dharmendra's Veeru in Sholay (1975).

questions.q: Which writer duo crafted many of the angry young man scripts that made Amitabh Bachchan a superstar?
questions.explanation: Screenwriters Salim Khan and Javed Akhtar, known as Salim-Javed, wrote Zanjeer, Deewaar and other defining Bachchan films.

questions.q: Which popular television quiz show has Amitabh Bachchan hosted on Indian television since 2000?
questions.explanation: Amitabh Bachchan has been the long-running host of Kaun Banega Crorepati, the Indian version of Who Wants to Be a Millionaire, since 2000.

questions.q: In the 1975 film Deewaar, Amitabh Bachchan played a character who becomes which of the following?
options: A smuggler and dock worker turned crime boss
questions.explanation: In Deewaar, Amitabh Bachchan played Vijay, a dock worker who rises into the smuggling underworld, opposite his policeman brother.

questions.q: Which Indian actress did Amitabh Bachchan marry in 1973?
questions.explanation: Amitabh Bachchan married actress Jaya Bhaduri in 1973, and they frequently co-starred together.

questions.q: What is the name of Amitabh Bachchan's son, who is also a Hindi film actor?
questions.explanation: Amitabh Bachchan's son Abhishek Bachchan is an actor who debuted in 2000 and later co-starred with his father in several films.

questions.q: In which Indian city was Amitabh Bachchan born?
questions.explanation: Amitabh Bachchan was born in Allahabad (now Prayagraj), Uttar Pradesh, in 1942.

questions.q: Amitabh Bachchan's father was a celebrated Hindi poet. What was his name?
questions.explanation: Amitabh Bachchan's father was Harivansh Rai Bachchan, the renowned poet who wrote Madhushala.

questions.q: In which 1969 film did Amitabh Bachchan make his acting debut?
questions.explanation: Amitabh Bachchan made his film debut in Khwaja Ahmad Abbas's Saat Hindustani (1969).

questions.q: For which 1971 Hrishikesh Mukherjee film, in which he starred opposite Rajesh Khanna, did Amitabh Bachchan win his first Filmfare Award?
questions.explanation: Amitabh Bachchan won the Filmfare Best Supporting Actor award for his role as Dr. Bhaskar Banerjee in Anand (1971).

questions.q: Which 1982 film's on-set accident left Amitabh Bachchan critically injured, triggering nationwide prayers for his recovery?
questions.explanation: Amitabh Bachchan suffered a near-fatal intestinal injury during a fight scene while filming Coolie (released 1983) in 1982.

questions.q: In the 1978 multi-starrer Don, Amitabh Bachchan played a dual role of a feared crime boss and which other character?
options: A simple paan-chewing commoner named Vijay
questions.explanation: In Don (1978), Amitabh Bachchan played both the gangster Don and the look-alike commoner Vijay who impersonates him.

questions.q: Which constituency did Amitabh Bachchan win as a Member of Parliament in the 1984 Indian general election?
questions.explanation: Amitabh Bachchan won the Allahabad Lok Sabha seat in 1984, defeating H. N. Bahuguna, before resigning from politics in 1987.

questions.q: For which 2005 Sanjay Leela Bhansali film did Amitabh Bachchan win a Filmfare Best Actor award playing a teacher of a deaf-blind girl?
questions.explanation: Amitabh Bachchan won acclaim and a Filmfare Best Actor award for playing teacher Debraj Sahai in Black (2005).

questions.q: In the 2000 Yash Raj Films production Mohabbatein, Amitabh Bachchan played a stern figure opposite Shah Rukh Khan. What was his character's role?
questions.explanation: In Mohabbatein (2000), Amitabh Bachchan played Narayan Shankar, the rigid headmaster of the Gurukul college.

questions.q: Amitabh Bachchan's distinctive baritone voice was famously used for narration in which 2001 epic film by Ashutosh Gowariker?
questions.explanation: Amitabh Bachchan provided the opening voice-over narration for the Oscar-nominated film Lagaan (2001).

questions.q: In which 1977 Manmohan Desai film did Amitabh Bachchan play one of three brothers separated in childhood and raised in different religions?
questions.explanation: In Amar Akbar Anthony (1977), Amitabh Bachchan played Anthony Gonsalves, the Christian-raised brother of the title trio.

questions.q: In which 1988 film did Amitabh Bachchan play a vigilante and deliver the famous line 'Rishte mein toh hum tumhare baap lagte hain'?
questions.explanation: The iconic line 'Rishte mein toh hum tumhare baap lagte hain, naam hai Shahenshah' is from Shahenshah (1988).

questions.q: Which 1982 Ramesh Sippy film paired Amitabh Bachchan with veteran actor Dilip Kumar in a father-son conflict?
questions.explanation: Shakti (1982) cast Amitabh Bachchan as the son of Dilip Kumar's upright police officer, the only film to pair the two stars.

questions.q: Before becoming an actor, Amitabh Bachchan worked in which profession in Kolkata?
options: Freight broker at a shipping firm
questions.explanation: Amitabh Bachchan worked as a freight broker for the shipping firm Bird & Co. in Calcutta before entering films.

questions.q: In the 1973 Hrishikesh Mukherjee film Abhimaan, Amitabh Bachchan played a singer whose career is eclipsed by his wife. Which actress played his on-screen wife?
questions.explanation: In Abhimaan (1973), Amitabh Bachchan starred opposite his real-life wife Jaya Bhaduri, who played the more successful singer Uma.

questions.q: For which 1990 film did Amitabh Bachchan win his first National Film Award for Best Actor, playing the don Vijay Dinanath Chauhan?
questions.explanation: Amitabh Bachchan won his first National Film Award for Best Actor for Agneepath (1990), playing Vijay Dinanath Chauhan.

questions.q: What was the name of the media and entertainment company launched by Amitabh Bachchan in 1995 that later ran into severe financial trouble?
questions.explanation: Amitabh Bachchan launched ABCL (Amitabh Bachchan Corporation Limited) in 1995, which collapsed financially and left him heavily in debt before being renamed AB Corp.

questions.q: In which year was Amitabh Bachchan honoured with the Dadasaheb Phalke Award, India's highest cinema honour?
questions.explanation: Amitabh Bachchan received the Dadasaheb Phalke Award for 2019, presented in 2021 due to pandemic delays.

questions.q: In the 2009 film Paa, Amitabh Bachchan played a 12-year-old boy suffering from which condition, opposite his real-life son cast as his father?
questions.explanation: In Paa (2009), Amitabh Bachchan played Auro, a child with the rapid-ageing disorder progeria, while Abhishek Bachchan played his father.

questions.q: Which 2015 Shoojit Sircar film about an eccentric Bengali patriarch obsessed with digestion earned Amitabh Bachchan a National Film Award for Best Actor?
questions.explanation: Amitabh Bachchan won the National Film Award for Best Actor for playing Bhashkor Banerjee in Piku (2015).

questions.q: Which 1979 Yash Chopra film, set against the backdrop of a coal mine disaster, featured Amitabh Bachchan as a disgraced man named Vijay seeking redemption?
questions.explanation: In Kaala Patthar (1979), Amitabh Bachchan played Vijay Pal Singh, a former ship captain turned coal miner seeking redemption.
